clusters.root_cause_summary: The stair centroid lies in a navmesh-disconnected component, so pointnav never converges and Reach_stair_centroid remains False on every attempt. The stair-traversal phase (is_robot_in_stair_map_fast) is never entered; the agent cycles through stair-frontier disables and re-exploration without ever switching floors.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Identical: both show Reach_stair_centroid: False across all logged attempts; both disable the stair frontier multiple times via the same two failure codes ('no movement or reaching start', 'Pointnav policy stopped'); candidate_1 patch produces identical mode-sequence fingerprints in both scenes, confirming the traversal-phase guard is never reached. Different: q3zU7Yy5E5s targets an upstair centroid with 35+ consecutive failures and suffers a 75-step episode regression from the off-target patch; qyAac8rV8Zk targets a downstair centroid with min_dis_to_downstair oscillating 166→160 pixels across all attempts (no monotone convergence). The direction of the blocked stair (up vs. down) differs, but the mechanism is structurally identical.
clusters.transfer_notes: Applying the premature-stair-climb-success guard (candidate_1) to both scenes confirmed that failure occurs entirely within get_close_to_stair, before is_robot_in_stair_map_fast is ever evaluated — the guard is never triggered in either scene. The 75-step regression on q3zU7Yy5E5s also reveals that a slightly shifted centroid (from patch side-effects altering reset timing) is equally unreachable, ruling out coordinate jitter as a viable fix and narrowing the target to connected-navmesh entry-point selection.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: stair_centroid_navmesh_reachability_precheck_before_get_close_to_stair combined with alternative_stair_entry_point_sampling_from_connected_navmesh_boundary: before committing to get_close_to_stair, run a navmesh reachability query from the agent's current position to the candidate centroid; if unreachable, resample candidate entry points by walking the navmesh boundary of the stair footprint until a connected point is found, or trigger an early floor-skip if no reachable entry exists

clusters.root_cause_summary: The stair is physically traversed successfully (floor_step resets to 0 at step 198), but the upper floor's frontier set collapses to only two frontiers within 16 post-switch steps, and the goal object lies in a navmesh-disconnected region that repeated floor_reinits and NOQUIT rescues cannot bridge.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single scene. Defining fingerprint: (a) confirmed stair traversal via floor_step=0 reset, (b) post-switch frontier supply at steps 199–215 limited to 0.2m and 2.2m — exhausted before step 216, (c) five floor_reinits and two NOQUIT rescues all fail because the bed is in a disconnected navmesh component. Candidate_1 produced bit-for-bit identical fingerprints, confirming no lever acting before or during stair traversal can address this failure.
clusters.transfer_notes: No cross-scene transfer applicable (single scene). However, the structural analogy to the navmesh_disconnected_stair_centroid cluster is notable: both failure classes are rooted in navmesh disconnection, and a shared navmesh-reachability diagnostic utility (precheck before committing to a target) could serve both clusters. Here the disconnected entity is the goal region rather than the stair centroid, but the fix shape — detect disconnection early and skip or expand the search scope — is the same.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: navmesh_connectivity_precheck_for_floor_skip combined with post_floor_switch_frontier_seeding_radius and DP10_DP11_value_map_upper_floor_coverage: at floor switch, assess whether the newly activated floor has sufficient connected frontier coverage; if the frontier set is sparse (< threshold) or the goal semantic region appears disconnected from reachable space, either seed additional frontiers at an expanded radius from the switch point or trigger a skip to an alternative floor before exhausting the step budget on dead-end reinits

forbidden_moves.reason: Ruled out for both q3zU7Yy5E5s and qyAac8rV8Zk; LLM response parsing operates on frontier selection, not on pointnav convergence to the stair centroid — the stair-traversal phase that DP7 influences is never entered in either scene.
Ruled out for both scenes; LLM prompt construction cannot alter navmesh geometry or make a disconnected centroid reachable.
Ruled out for both scenes; same rationale as DP5 — prompt content has no causal path to pointnav convergence failure.
Ruled out for both scenes; the LLM trigger threshold governs frontier ranking, not the get_close_to_stair loop that is failing in both episodes.
Ruled out for both scenes; multi-floor LLM selection is downstream of a successful floor switch — which never occurs in either scene.
Ruled out for both scenes; floor-switch interval gating is irrelevant when the stair centroid is geometrically unreachable and the floor switch is never triggered.

Explicitly confirmed off-target by candidate_1 experiments on both scenes: is_robot_in_stair_map_fast is never evaluated because get_close_to_stair never converges; the guard condition is structurally unreachable. Identical fingerprints in both candidates proved zero behavioral effect.
Ruled out for XB4GS9ShBRE; LLM frontier parsing cannot generate new frontiers in a navmesh-disconnected upper-floor region.
Ruled out for XB4GS9ShBRE; LLM prompts have no effect on post-switch frontier supply or navmesh connectivity of the goal region.
Ruled out for XB4GS9ShBRE; same rationale as DP5.
Ruled out for XB4GS9ShBRE; LLM trigger threshold does not affect the structural scarcity of upper-floor frontiers or navmesh connectivity.
Ruled out for XB4GS9ShBRE; multi-floor LLM selection is downstream of the frontier exhaustion event and cannot retroactively expand exploration coverage.
Ruled out for XB4GS9ShBRE; adjusting floor-switch interval does not fix the sparse/disconnected upper-floor frontier set that appears immediately after the switch.
Ruled out for XB4GS9ShBRE; stair carrot waypoint distance is irrelevant after the stair has already been successfully traversed (floor_step=0 confirmed at step 198).

Confirmed off-target for XB4GS9ShBRE by candidate_1: T5_STAIR_CLIMB_EVAL SUCCESS fired at paused_step=20, which already exceeds the MIN_STAIR_STEPS=15 threshold, so the guard never activated and produced zero behavioral change (identical fingerprints across both candidates).
